instruction: Leave the bedroom and take a left in the hallway. Go straight and go into the room that is the second on the left. Stop in the doorway to the room.

system_prompt:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"Leave the bedroom and take a left in the hallway. Go straight and go into the room that is the second on the left. Stop in the doorway to the room. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
first_prompt: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tool_schemas.name: observe
tool_schemas.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tool_schemas.name: look_around
tool_schemas.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tool_schemas.name: step
tool_schemas.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

text: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I'll start by observing my surroundings.
I'm in the bedroom. I can see a bed and a doorway on the right side leading out (to the hallway). Let me scan around to understand the layout.
The exit to the hallway is ahead of me (the doorway on the right side of the ahead view, leading into a bright hallway). Let me move toward that doorway.
I've drifted toward a wall. The doorway to the hallway is to my right. Let me turn right to face the exit doorway.

Now I can see the hallway ahead, with an open area and railing. The door is to my left. The hallway opens up on the right. Let me turn a bit more right to face the hallway opening and move forward through the doorway.
I can see the doorway leading out to the hallway with a railing area (stairs) ahead. I need to move forward through this doorway into the hallway. Let me proceed forward.

I'm now at the doorway threshold looking into the hallway. There's a stair railing ahead. The hallway extends left and right. The instruction says to "take a left in the hallway." Let me look around to understand the hallway layout better.

Behind me (+180°) is the bedroom I came from. So the hallway is ahead of me. I need to first fully exit into the hallway, then take a left. 

Looking ahead, there's a railing (stairwell) directly ahead and the hallway continues to the right. Let me first step forward to fully enter the hallway, being careful of the railing/stairs.

The ahead view shows a railing directly ahead with an open hallway. Let me move forward a bit to clear the doorway.

Now I'm in the hallway landing. I see a stair railing in the center/left, and the hallway continues. I need to figure out which way is "left" in the hallway. 

The instruction: leave bedroom, take a left in the hallway. When I exited the bedroom I was facing the hallway. Now I see the hallway landing with a stairwell (railing) on the left and the hallway extending. Let me look at the left direction more carefully - there seems to be an opening on the right side and hallway going left.

Let me turn left to check what's there.

Turning left ran me into a wall (0.4/0.5 clearance ahead). The hallway continues to the right (the stairwell railing area). Let me reconsider. When I exited the bedroom, "ahead" showed the stairwell/hallway landing. 

Let me turn back to understand the hallway direction. The hallway seems to run to the right from the landing. Let me turn right to look down the main hallway.

Now I can see the hallway extending forward with doorways. This is the main hallway direction. There's a stairwell railing on the left. The hallway goes forward with doorways along it. 

The instruction says "take a left in the hallway" then "go into the room that is the second on the left." So after taking a left, I go down the hallway and the second door on the left is my goal.

Let me move down this hallway and observe the doorways. Let me proceed forward.
I'm in a narrower part of the hallway now. There's a doorway/opening on the left (clearance 0.4). Let me look around to identify the doorways along this hallway.

Now I understand the layout better. The "left" (+270°) view shows the hallway with the stairwell railing and it's the main hallway direction with the skylights. The "behind" (+180°) shows the bedroom I came from.

So my current "ahead" is a doorway/room. Let me reconsider the whole navigation.

When I left the bedroom, the hallway runs perpendicular. The instruction says take a LEFT in the hallway. Looking at the left view now (+270°), that's the direction of the main hallway with skylights that continues. 

Actually, I think I've turned too much. Let me reconsider: The left view (+270°) shows the open hallway extending with a doorway on the far left. That's the direction I should travel. Let me turn to face that hallway.

Now I'm facing down the main hallway with the stair railing on the right. There's a doorway on the left. Let me reconsider the full path.

I left the bedroom and I'm now in the hallway. The instruction is to take a left and go straight, then enter the second room on the left. 

I see on the left there's a doorway (the darker opening). As I go forward along this hallway, I'll pass doors on the left. Let me move forward to explore.

Now I can see a doorway on the left (an open room with a bathroom/white fixtures visible). This is the first door on the left. The hallway continues forward with the railing on the right. Let me note this as the first door on the left, and continue to find the second door.

Let me move forward past this first door.
I've now passed the first door on the left (the room with the bathroom, now behind-left of me). I can see the hallway continues forward. Let me continue and look for the second door on the left.

I can see the hallway continues, with a doorway ahead on the left side (near the center of the image, there's an opening). Let me look at the left side more carefully. There appears to be a door on the far left too. Let me continue forward.

Now I can see clearly. There are two doorways on the left:
- One on the far left (with an open room visible)
- One ahead in the center-left leading into a room (looks like a bedroom with bed visible)

Wait, let me reconsider. The door on the far left is the first door on the left. Actually I already passed the first door (bathroom). Let me look around to reorient and count the doors properly.

Now I have a clearer picture. Looking at the views:
- **left (+270°)**: A doorway into a bathroom (I see a vanity/sink). This is a door on the left.
- **ahead (0°)**: Hallway continues, with a doorway on the far left and a doorway in center-left.
- **behind (+180°)**: The hallway I came from.

Let me reconsider the door count. Behind me is the direction I came from. As I walked forward:
1. First door on left = the bathroom room I passed earlier
2. This bathroom to my left now (+270°) could be a second door OR the same. 

Actually, the layout is confusing. Let me re-examine. The instruction wants the second door on the left. In the ahead view there's a doorway on the far-left with a room, and another opening. Let me focus. 

The door immediately to my left (+270°) is a bathroom. Ahead there's another doorway on the left. Let me carefully count from when I started down the hallway:
- Door #1 (left): the bathroom-looking room I passed
- Door #2 (left): this current bathroom to my left

Hmm, but I saw the first room also had white fixtures (bathroom). Let me move forward slightly and look at the ahead-left doorway which shows a bedroom.

Now I can see two clear doorways on the left:
- Far left doorway: a bedroom (I can see a bed with white bedding)
- Center doorway: another room (looks like a bathroom/room with bed visible)

Let me reconsider the counting. When I started down the hallway from the landing:
- First door on left: the bathroom I passed 
- Then this current section has more doors

This is getting complex. Let me re-establish: which was the first door on the left after taking the left turn in the hallway?

Looking back at my path: After leaving the bedroom, I turned into the hallway heading in the direction with skylights. The first room I passed on the left was the bathroom (with white fixtures). Now I'm seeing more doorways.

The bathroom to my immediate left (+270° earlier) — I've now moved past it. Ahead-left there are two doorways. Let me look at them. The far-left is clearly a bedroom. Let me count: the bathroom was door 1, this far-left bedroom appears to be door 2.

Let me move to get a clearer view of these doorways and their positions.

Now I can see a doorway on the left leading to a room (I can see what looks like a bathroom with a toilet). Let me reconsider my whole path once more by thinking carefully.

Actually, I realize I need to be systematic. Let me reconsider the door counting from the moment I turned left in the hallway. The bathroom I passed first was the FIRST door on the left. 

Now, this doorway on my left here — I need to determine if it's the first or second. Let me turn back to face down the hallway and check what's ahead versus what I passed.

Let me turn right back to hallway direction.

Now I'm facing back down the hallway direction. I can see clearly:
- On the far LEFT: a doorway to a bedroom (bed with white bedding visible)
- In the CENTER-left: another doorway to a room

Let me trace the doors I've passed on the left since turning into the hallway:
1. First door (left): the bathroom near the stairwell landing
2. The far-left bedroom here would be door #2

Wait, but there seem to be multiple doorways bunched together. Let me carefully identify. The far-left doorway (bedroom) and the center doorway. 

Given this is the second "cluster," and I already passed the first bathroom, the far-left bedroom door here is likely the SECOND door on the left. But I want to be careful. Let me examine the far-left doorway first by moving toward it.

Actually, let me reconsider positions. The hallway I'm traveling has a stairwell on the right side. On the left there were doors. Let me approach the leftmost doorway (bedroom) and count.

I turned too far left and now I'm facing a wall with a doorway to the right of center. Let me turn back to reorient. I see the doorway (bedroom) is now to my right. 

Let me step back and think about this more carefully. I'll turn to get a clean look at the two doorways.

Now I see a doorway on the left leading to a room with a vanity/bathroom setup. This is the same bathroom-type room I saw at +270° earlier. 

I'm getting confused by turning. Let me be more systematic. Let me do a full look_around from this position to map all doorways.

Now I have a good map. Let me analyze:
- **left (+270°)**: This is the hallway going back toward the stairwell landing (I see the railing and hallway). This is the direction I came FROM originally.
- **behind (+180°)**: Shows a wall with closet doors and the stairwell railing.
- **right (+90°)**: Shows the two doorways — far-left bedroom (with bed) and center doorway to a room.
- **ahead (0°)**: The bathroom room with vanity.

So my orientation is confused. Let me reconstruct. The **left (+270°)** view shows the hallway with railing extending back — this is the main hallway leading back toward where I entered. 

Let me reconsider the ENTIRE journey. I entered the hallway from the bedroom, and the hallway with skylights/railing was the path. I've been traveling along it. The rooms are:
- The bathroom with vanity (ahead of me now)
- The bedroom (to my right +90°)

Given the instruction structure — take left in hallway, go straight, second room on left — I've traveled a good distance. The two prominent doors here (bathroom and bedroom) are candidates. 

Let me reconsider door counting. The main hallway runs from the landing (where I came in) forward. Along it, on the LEFT side as you walk away from the landing:
1. First door on left = bathroom (near landing) 
2. Second door on left = this bathroom/bedroom cluster

Actually I think I've overcomplicated this. Let me face down the hallway in my travel direction properly. The +270° view shows hallway with railing — but that's looking back. So my forward travel direction should be opposite.

Let me turn to face the direction where the hallway continues forward (away from landing). Currently ahead (0°) is the vanity room. Let me reconsider — the hallway probably continues past these rooms.

Let me turn to face +270° (the hallway with railing) reversed, i.e., turn around. Actually let me just re-orient to travel down the hallway. The railing hallway at +270° leads back to start. So forward is +90° area? No.

Let me step forward into the current view to see the bathroom, since ahead is a doorway.

I'm hitting a wall - this is a wall between me and the room. The doorway to the bathroom is to my left. Let me step back and reconsider - I moved into a corner. 

Let me back up and reconsider the whole situation carefully. Let me turn left to see the doorway.

Now I can see a doorway with an open door leading into a room (bathroom with tiled floor). This is a doorway on this side. 

I've been going back and forth. Let me take a completely fresh, systematic approach. I've clearly gotten disoriented. Let me carefully reconstruct my position.

The key landmark is the stairwell railing which marks the main upstairs hallway. Rooms open off this hallway. Let me reposition myself in the center of the hallway and count doors carefully going one direction.

Let me first back away from this door and get into the hallway center. Let me turn around to face the open hallway.

Now I see the stairwell landing area with skylights and a closet (double doors) on the left, and the stair railing on the right. This is the central landing area. 

Let me establish a clear mental map. This upstairs area has:
- A stairwell (railing) 
- The bedroom I started in
- Several rooms/doors

The instruction: "Leave the bedroom and take a left in the hallway. Go straight and go into the room that is the second on the left."

I need to trace this from the bedroom exit. Let me do a look_around here to fully map this landing.

Now I have a much clearer view. Let me analyze:
- **ahead (0°)**: Closet with double doors on left, stairwell railing on right — this is the landing/top of stairs.
- **right (+90°)**: Hallway extends with a door on the right, railing on left.
- **behind (+180°)**: The bathroom door (open door, tiled room).
- **left (+270°)**: Two doorways! One on the far left (leads to hallway with a room) and one on the center-right that shows a bedroom (bed with orange/yellow bedding visible).

The **left (+270°)** view is very informative — it shows the hallway continuing with doorways. This looks like the main hallway direction.

Let me reconsider the whole route. I started in a bedroom (yellow bedding). I exited to the hallway/landing. Now, the instruction says take a left. 

I think the cleanest interpretation: The hallway runs along the stairwell. The left+270 view shows a hallway with a bedroom visible. Let me go toward that (+270°) and count doors on the left.

Let me turn to face +270° and travel that way.

Now I'm facing the +270° direction. I can see:
- On the far LEFT: a doorway (dark, another hallway/room)
- On the RIGHT: a doorway into a bedroom with yellow/orange bedding

Wait — the bedroom with yellow bedding on the right looks like it could be my starting bedroom or a similar one. Let me look at the far-left doorway. 

Actually, I realize this is the hallway. The doorway on the right leads to a bedroom. The doorway on the left (dark) leads elsewhere. 

Let me reconsider. I've spent a lot of budget being confused. Let me approach this methodically. The instruction endpoint is "the doorway to the room that is second on the left." 

Let me move forward down this hallway (center is open at 2.2m) and observe doors on my left as I go.

Now I can see two doorways clearly:
- LEFT doorway: leads to a bedroom (I see a bed with white/light bedding on the far left)
- RIGHT doorway: leads to a bedroom with yellow/orange bedding

I'm at a junction. Let me look around to fully understand this spot.

OK this is a dead-end area between two bedrooms. Let me analyze:
- **ahead (0°)**: wall with a bedroom on the left (white bed) and bedroom on the right (yellow bed)
- **right (+90°)**: The landing with closet and stairwell railing
- **behind (+180°)**: The main hallway with railing and doors (this connects back toward where I came)
- **left (+270°)**: wall/corner with a bedroom visible on the far right

The bedroom with yellow bedding (ahead-right / behind's rooms) — this is likely my STARTING bedroom. Wait, my starting bedroom had yellow patterned bedding. Yes! So the yellow bedroom is where I started.

So this means I've circled around. Let me reconsider the geometry completely:
- My starting bedroom = yellow bedding room
- I exited it into the hallway/landing area

The instruction: leave bedroom, take LEFT in hallway, go straight, second room on left.

The main hallway is the one with the stairwell railing (behind me at +180° and the earlier hallway). Let me reconsider from the very start.

When I first left the bedroom, I was facing the hallway. Taking a "left" means turning left. Let me go back to the bedroom door and redo this properly. Actually, the yellow bedroom is right here (ahead-right). Let me go stand at its doorway to reset.

The yellow bedroom door is at ahead(0°) on the right side. Let me face it.

Now I can see the yellow-bedding bedroom through the doorway on the left, and the closet (double doors) on the right. 

Let me confirm this is my starting bedroom by stepping toward it.

I'm at the doorway to the yellow-bedding bedroom. This is my STARTING bedroom (yellow patterned bedding matches). 

Now let me do the instruction cleanly. I'm at the bedroom doorway. To "leave the bedroom and take a left in the hallway," I need to exit and turn left. Let me first face into the bedroom to establish the "leaving" orientation, then turn around.

Actually, I'm currently outside the bedroom looking in. When I "leave the bedroom," I'd be facing OUT (away from the bed). So let me turn around 180° to face the way I'd be walking when leaving.

Now I'm facing OUT of the bedroom (as if leaving it). I can see the hallway. There's a doorway ahead-center, and the hallway extends to the left (2.9m clearance on left, stairwell railing visible on far left).

"Take a left in the hallway" — so I turn LEFT. The left direction has the hallway extending (2.9m clearance). Let me turn left.

Now I've turned left and I'm facing the landing area — closet double doors on the left, stairwell railing on the right. This is the landing at the top of the stairs, not really a hallway to go straight down.

Hmm. This suggests taking a "left" from the bedroom leads to the landing with the stairwell. That's not a hallway with doors. Let me reconsider.

Perhaps I turned the wrong way. When leaving the bedroom facing out, the "hallway" runs left-right. Let me reconsider: the bedroom door faces into the landing. The actual HALLWAY (with multiple doors) was the one I explored earlier with the railing on one side and doors on the other.

Let me reconsider: Earlier from the landing, the hallway with the skylights and doors ran in one direction. Let me look at where that hallway is from here. Let me look around.

Now this is very revealing! Let me analyze:
- **ahead (0°)**: Landing with closet doors + stairwell railing.
- **right (+90°)**: A hallway extending with a doorway on the right side! (This is a hallway going straight.)
- **behind (+180°)**: A bedroom with WHITE/gray bedding (a DIFFERENT bedroom than yellow!)
- **left (+270°)**: The YELLOW bedding bedroom (my start).

So there are two bedrooms adjacent: yellow (left/+270) and white (behind/+180). 

Wait — I need to reconsider which is my starting bedroom. My starting bedroom had YELLOW patterned bedding. That's at +270° (left).

So actually I'm positioned between the two bedrooms. The +270° yellow bedroom is my start.

Let me reset properly. I'll face out of the YELLOW bedroom. The yellow bedroom is at +270°. To leave it, I face out from it, meaning I look from inside toward the door. The door faces +270° from here... 

This is getting complicated. Let me think about it differently using the hallway. The **right (+90°)** view clearly shows a HALLWAY extending straight with a door on the right. This must be the main hallway with the doors I explored earlier.

The instruction: leave bedroom (yellow), take a LEFT into hallway, go straight, second room on left.

The yellow bedroom door — when I exit it, which way is the hallway? Let me go into the yellow bedroom and turn around to see its exit orientation. Let me face +270° (into yellow bedroom).
I bumped into the door frame. The yellow bedroom is to my right. Let me turn right to enter it and confirm.

I'm wedged against a wall/corner. The bedroom is to my left. I need to back up. Let me reconsider — I'm burning budget. Let me step back and take a cleaner approach.

Let me back away from this corner by turning and moving into open space.

OK I'm back in the open landing facing the closet and stairwell railing. I have 188 steps left, plenty.

Let me make a firm decision. Based on my exploration, the key hallway with multiple doors runs along the stairwell. Earlier (tool calls 17-45) I walked down a hallway with the railing on the right and passed doors on the left: first a bathroom, then a bedroom-cluster.

Let me reconsider the instruction with what I know:
- Yellow bedroom = start (has a door to the landing)
- White bedroom = adjacent
- The landing has stairs
- A hallway with skylights runs with railing, and has doors: bathroom (first left), then more rooms

Given "take a left in the hallway, go straight, second room on left" — the most sensible reading: exit bedroom, the hallway runs, turn left, then the 2nd door on the left is the goal.

Earlier when I walked the hallway (facing away from landing, railing on right), I passed: door 1 (bathroom near landing), and then reached a cluster with a bedroom door and a bathroom-vanity door. The 2nd door on the left would be that bedroom.

Let me navigate back to that hallway. From here (facing closet/railing), the hallway extends. Let me look at the +90/right to find the hallway with doors. Let me turn right toward the hallway.

Now I can see a hallway. There's a doorway ahead-right (leading to a room with what looks like a chair/furniture) and a door on the far left. The hallway extends. Let me proceed forward down this hallway to reach the door area I identified earlier.
I see a bedroom on the right (with a bed). There's a doorway/room on the right (3.0m clearance). I'm getting close to walls ahead. Let me look around to reorient.

Now I understand better:
- **ahead (0°)** and **right (+90°)**: a bedroom (white/gray bed) — I'm at its doorway.
- **behind (+180°)**: the landing with closet doors and hallway.
- **left (+270°)**: the STAIRWELL landing with railing and skylights, and hallway continuing on the right.

The **left (+270°)** view shows the main hallway with the stairwell railing — the hallway with the skylights I explored. This is the "go straight" hallway.

I've been struggling. Let me commit to a clear interpretation. Given the layout:
- Two bedrooms are near the landing (yellow=start, white)
- The main hallway runs along the stairwell with the bathroom and other rooms

Let me reconsider from the original: When I FIRST left the bedroom (tool call 9-16), I entered the hallway and there was a railing ahead. I turned and went down a hallway passing: a bathroom on the left, then more doors. 

The 2nd door on the left is most plausibly the room I saw at doorway around tool call 33-46 (bedroom with a bed, and the bathroom-vanity). 

Given budget constraints (25 tool calls, 155 steps), let me make a decision. The white-bedding bedroom right here (ahead/+90) is a strong candidate for a "room." 

Actually — let me reconsider the door counting most carefully. The main hallway with railing had along its LEFT (as walking away from landing): first the bathroom, then the vanity-bathroom, then bedrooms. 

Let me go to the main hallway (+270°) and walk it counting doors on the left. Let me face +270°.

Now I'm facing the stairwell landing. The stair railing is in the center. The hallway continues to the RIGHT (past the railing). There's a doorway on the far left too, and the hallway extends to the right with a door visible on the far right.

The "go straight" hallway is to the right side of the railing. Let me navigate around the railing to the right where the hallway continues. Let me turn slightly right and move along the hallway on the right side of the railing.

Now I can see the hallway extending forward, with the stairwell railing on the left and a doorway ahead on the right/center (the open door). This is the hallway "going straight." 

This matches what I saw at the very beginning after leaving the bedroom (tool calls 16-19). Let me walk down this hallway now and count doors on the LEFT. Currently the left side has the railing then wall. Let me proceed forward.

text:
I need to commit now. I see the hallway continues to the left (open, 2.3m) and there's a doorway on the right (0.5m, an open door). 

Let me reconsider the door count in this hallway going straight from the landing:
- The hallway extends and the doors I passed earlier on the LEFT were: bathroom (1st), then a bedroom/room (2nd).

Right now, the hallway opens to the left. Let me proceed and take the hallway. The left is open — this is where the hallway with doors continues. Let me move forward into the hallway.

text:
I see an open door on the RIGHT (with a door slab visible), and the hallway continues ahead with the railing on the left. This is the hallway with doors. 

I'm now in the main hallway. There's a door on the right. The doors I need to count are on the LEFT though. Let me continue forward — this is the same hallway where I earlier passed the bathroom (1st left door) and then a room cluster (2nd left).

Let me move forward and look for the left-side doors.
